mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. The key trade-off is the complexity of setup versus the automation and consistency it provides.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which will manage the lifecycle of your clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

```

> This command deploys Cluster API in the kube-system namespace.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if clusters are not properly deleted first.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This removes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster state. This step involves upgrading the Cluster API components and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o json > backup-clusters.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup current cluster state.
> This command upgrades Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster resource.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates a misconfiguration in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig or the API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly set up and points to the right cluster context.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your created clusters and their statuses.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and Helm installed.
